premise: A remake package compares a childhood group photo against speculative 2026 outcomes by alternating child name cards with future stills, while omitting the final TikTok Lite end slate by default.
plot_type: static montage of intro card, child name cards, and future cards with editable Shotstack text overlays

cast.role: child subject in the intro group and first name card
cast.description: Curly-haired girl from the left side of the group photo.

cast.role: child subject in the intro group and second name card
cast.description: Straight black-haired girl from the group photo.

cast.role: child subject in the intro group and third name card
cast.description: Blonde girl with glasses from the group photo.

cast.role: child subject in the intro group and fourth name card
cast.description: Braided Black girl from the group photo.

continuity_rules: This is not a single-lead influencer video. Each card is scene-local and identity-specific to the source montage.
The opening group card and the four child close-ups all come from the same Chinatown photo.
Each future '2026' card is a different still image with no guaranteed shared location, wardrobe, or demographic continuity beyond montage placement.
The final 2026 card after Amanda is ambiguous in the source and must remain review-required before any generation decisions.
Editorial text such as '2006 vs 2026', child-name labels, and centered '2026' cards should be rebuilt as editable Shotstack text overlays instead of being baked into remake base media.
TikTok Lite watermark, username, and the final platform end slate are not creative content to regenerate by default; omit the terminal platform slate unless the user explicitly requests it.

supporting_character_policy.rationale: The montage includes several distinct named people rather than one lead plus supporting cast. Because pairings are editorial rather than narrative, this package remains review-required.

scene_arc.beat: Introduce the 2006 vs 2026 comparison with the four-child group photo and an editable centered title.
Identify Jessica with an editable centered name label.
Show the first implied 2026 outcome with an editable year label.
Identify Lina with an editable centered name label.
Show the second implied 2026 outcome with an editable year label.
Identify Rachel with an editable centered name label.
Show the third implied 2026 outcome with an editable year label.
Identify Amanda with an editable centered name label.
Show the fourth implied 2026 outcome, preserving the pairing ambiguity while keeping the year label editable.
